meta.purpose: Define the axioms, ontology, and frameworks that govern bug diagnosis judgment.
meta.load_condition: Always loaded when this domain is active.

axioms.one_sentence: A fix without reproduction is a guess.
axioms.full_statement: If the agent cannot reproduce the bug under controlled conditions, any fix is an unverified hypothesis. Reproduction is the foundation of all diagnosis.
axioms.why: Fixing without reproduction addresses symptoms, not causes. The bug recurs or manifests elsewhere.

axioms.one_sentence: The first plausible hypothesis is rarely the correct one.
axioms.full_statement: The agent must generate multiple ranked, falsifiable hypotheses before testing any of them. Committing to the first hypothesis creates confirmation bias.
axioms.why: Agents that jump to the first explanation waste time confirming what they already believe instead of eliminating what they don't.

axioms.one_sentence: Changing more than one variable at a time destroys diagnostic signal.
axioms.full_statement: Each diagnostic step must isolate one variable. If two or more variables change simultaneously, the result cannot be attributed to either.
axioms.why: Simultaneous changes make it impossible to determine which change fixed (or caused) the observed behavior.

axioms.one_sentence: A regression test must exist before the fix is applied.
axioms.full_statement: The regression test captures the bug's reproduction condition. Without it, there is no proof the fix worked and no guard against recurrence.
axioms.why: Fixes that lack regression tests can silently regress. The bug reappears and no test catches it.

axioms.one_sentence: The feedback loop is the diagnosis.
axioms.full_statement: Without a feedback loop — a way to observe the bug's behavior on demand — the agent is debugging blind. Constructing the loop is the most important step, not the fix itself.
axioms.why: Agents that skip loop construction cannot verify any hypothesis and may 'fix' bugs that were never reproduced.

axioms.one_sentence: A symptom is not a cause.
axioms.full_statement: What the user reports (crash, error message, wrong output) is a symptom. The cause is the condition that produces the symptom. Diagnosis is the process of tracing from symptom to cause.
axioms.why: Treating symptoms as causes produces fixes that mask the bug locally while it persists systemically.

ontology.one_sentence: Reproduction is the smallest set of conditions that reliably triggers the bug.
ontology.essence: A reproduction is not 'sometimes it happens.' It is a deterministic or near-deterministic procedure that produces the observed failure on demand.
ontology.boundary: Intermittent bugs have probabilistic reproduction, not no reproduction. A reproduction rate of 1-in-100 is still reproduction if the conditions are specified.
ontology.trigger_signal: The agent is about to propose a fix but has not demonstrated that the bug can be triggered reliably.

ontology.one_sentence: A hypothesis is a falsifiable explanation of the root cause.
ontology.essence: A hypothesis must predict a specific observation if true and a different observation if false. 'Something is wrong with the data' is not a hypothesis.
ontology.boundary: A hypothesis is not a guess. A guess is an unstructured possibility. A hypothesis is a testable prediction with predicted outcomes.
ontology.trigger_signal: The agent is considering what might be wrong but has not stated a falsifiable prediction.

ontology.one_sentence: Instrumentation is temporary observation code added to capture runtime behavior at the diagnostic point.
ontology.essence: Instrumentation makes the invisible visible. It adds logging, tracing, or assertions at specific points to observe state during reproduction.
ontology.boundary: Instrumentation is not a fix. It must be removed after diagnosis. It is diagnostic scaffolding, not production code.
ontology.trigger_signal: The agent needs to observe runtime state that is not currently visible through existing logs or tests.

ontology.one_sentence: Minimal reproduction is the smallest possible case that still triggers the bug.
ontology.essence: If removing any element of the reproduction case stops the bug from occurring, it is minimal. Every element in the reproduction contributes to triggering the bug.
ontology.boundary: Minimal reproduction is not 'the simplest code that has a bug.' It is the simplest demonstration of this specific bug.
ontology.trigger_signal: The reproduction case involves many steps, and it's unclear which steps are essential.

ontology.one_sentence: A feedback loop is a mechanism that allows the agent to observe the bug's behavior on demand within seconds or minutes.
ontology.essence: The loop can be a failing test, a script that runs the relevant code, a browser interaction, or a HITL procedure. What matters is speed and reliability.
ontology.boundary: A feedback loop is not 'deploy and check in staging.' It must be fast enough to support iterative hypothesis testing.
ontology.trigger_signal: The agent cannot verify its hypotheses without manual intervention or long wait times.

frameworks.name: Diagnosis Loop
frameworks.when_to_use: When a bug, test failure, or runtime error needs to be fixed.
steps: Construct or identify a feedback loop that reproduces the bug on demand
Minimize the reproduction to the smallest case that still triggers the bug
Generate 3-5 ranked, falsifiable hypotheses about the root cause
Instrument to test one hypothesis at a time, changing one variable per step
Write a regression test that captures the reproduction condition
Apply the fix and verify the regression test passes
Remove instrumentation and document the root cause in the commit message

core_structure.via: Construct feedback loop → Minimize → Hypothesize → Instrument → Fix → Regression test

core_structure.to: Root cause (the condition producing the symptom)
core_structure.via: Trace the symptom backward through the execution path, testing one hypothesis at a time

stances: Never fix what you cannot reproduce.
One hypothesis at a time, one variable at a time.
The diagnosis is more valuable than the fix.
If the regression test doesn't exist, the bug isn't fixed — it's masked.
